Empires & Puzzles Ramona Review: The Ram-Riding Healer Blocker

Is Ramona's Ram-Riding antics still relevant in today's meta? We dive deep into this Champions family hero to see if her triple fiend healing denial can still lock down modern healers or if her stats have left her out to pasture.
Ramona, The Ram-Riding Healer Blocker: Healing Denial & Fiend Spam
Ramona is a 5-star Dark (Purple) hero from the
family, originally released in early 2024. She rides into battle on a giant ram, which is certainly a choice, bringing a kit focused on
single-target damage and extreme healing denial.

While she was once considered a top-tier sniper, the rapid power creep of late 2024 and 2025 has left her stat-line looking a bit like a "Classic" hero compared to the modern behemoths. However, her unique mechanic—summoning
healing-absorbing fiends on a single target—scales with the enemy's health, keeping her mechanically relevant even if her biceps aren't as big as they used to be.

Ramona's artwork is... distinct. She features a jockey-style outfit, complete with a helmet, riding a very enthusiastic-looking ram. It's a fun, quirky departure from the usual "edgy dark sorcerer" or "undead knight" aesthetic of the Purple element. The "Ram Turismo" vibe (as nicknamed by the community) adds a bit of levity to your roster, even if the look isn't for everyone.

She is effectively fighting with a 300-400 point deficit in every stat category compared to the latest releases. This makes her "squishy" by modern standards. She will struggle to survive hits from modern snipers and her direct damage won't dent the 1600+ defense of new tanks.
, her HP pool is decent enough that she isn't made of complete glass, but you shouldn't expect her to tank hits like she used to.

The direct damage is secondary now. 450% off a ~1200 attack stat is merely a "tickle" to modern heroes with 1600 defense.

Target Max HP: 3,000 (Common for LB2 modern heroes)
The target is effectively "Healing Immune" until they die or the fiends are cleansed.
Direct: 450% x 1259 Attack = ~5,665 raw damage value (before defense reduction). Against 1600 Def, this will hit for maybe 500-600 damage.
Fiend DoT: 3 fiends x 40% = 120% attack per turn. This is a nice bonus whittle, but won't kill anyone quickly.

Gaining +24% mana gen when receiving an ailment is fantastic. In a meta full of debuffs, this ensures she charges in 5 or even 4 ghosted tiles often.

Upon release, Ramona was a terror. "Ram Turismo" was a valid strategy, locking down key threats like Mother North or various tanks with un-healable fiends.
Her stats have fallen off a cliff compared to the "Construct", "Astral", and "Dragon" heroes. She is no longer a primary damage dealer.
She is no longer a general-purpose sniper. You bring Ramona for

: To shut down a high-value target that relies on healing (like a taunting tank or a reviver). If you can keep her alive long enough to fire once or twice, she can neutralize a key enemy. However, she is a liability on defense and risky on offense due to her low survivability.
Maximizes the damage of her snipe and, more importantly, the DoT of her fiends.
She is extremely fragile and might die before firing.
Keeps her alive longer to fire her skill multiple times. Since her main utility is the
of the fiends (healing absorb), not the damage, this ensures she actually delivers the payload.

Ramona's value is her utility (healing block). If she dies to a stiff breeze in turn 2, she does nothing. You need her to survive incidental AoE damage long enough to cast her skill on the enemy Healer/Tank.
135% Max HP healing absorb is practically insurmountable.
Charges in 6 tiles, allowing her to snipe a threat before they get going.
Heals herself for 300 boosted HP every time she fires.
Punishes enemies for placing ailments on her.
Extremely low stats compared to 2025/2026 heroes.
(which displace fiends) completely nullify her kit.
Only affects one enemy. In a rush meta, hitting one enemy might not be enough.
Excellent for neutralizing a specific annoying tank or healer in a tailored attack team.
Even faster firing means she can lock down a hero immediately.
Her speed allows her to pick off targets.
The healing absorb is useless since healing is already disabled, though the extra damage from fiends is okay.
Overwhelm the enemy with so many fiends that they cannot heal at all. Ramona picks off the cleanser/healer first.
Use a Taunt hero to protect Ramona's low stats. Let her charge up and surgically remove the enemy's ability to sustain themselves.
Convert fiends into minions. Hitting a team with Arco using Ramona is effectively giving the enemy free health.
Mega Minions often block or interact poorly with fiends, and newer heroes with "Immunity to Fiends" passives are a hard stop.
If you pulled Ramona, congratulations! Even with older stats, she is a

for map stages and early raiding. Being able to stop a boss from healing is an incredible tool for finishing hard PvE content. Level her up, but maybe hold off on Limit Breaking her until you are sure you need her for Wars.

Ramona is a "luxury tool" at this point. Do not use her on Defense—she will be farmed. On offense, she is a silver bullet against teams relying heavily on a single healer (like a super-tanky Toon Vivica). If you struggle against heavy stall/heal teams, she is worth the roster slot, but likely not worth the Alpha Aethers unless you really love the art.
"Ram Turismo alone sold me. She and Grimble are best buddies."
- A fan of the unique naming and synergy.
"She is broken... spawning 3 fiends each hitting really hard... B-R-O-K-E-N"
- A common sentiment upon her release, highlighting how oppressive 135% healing absorb felt at the time.
"Definitely the worst of new sport legends."
- A dissenter from the early days, proving you can't please everyone (though history proved her utility was strong).

Ramona is the definition of a "mechanic-heavy" hero. Her stats have faded into the background, but the sheer audacity of summoning three healing-sponges onto a single enemy ensures she still has a seat at the table in deep wars. She isn't the brute force hammer she used to be; she's now the scalpel you use to sever the enemy's lifeline.
A niche but powerful tool for your war chest. Keep her for the healers you just can't kill.
